sections.paragraph:
to be tender, soft (Arab. ركََّ, kindred to the root רָקַק No. I); figuratively-(a) to be delicate, Deu 28:56.-(b) to be weakened, contrite in mind, 2Ki 22:19 see Niph.; used of soft words, Psa 55:22.

Niphal, fut. יֵרַךְ to be weakened (broken), of the mind or heart (לֵב) to become timid, Deu 20:3 Isa 7:4 Jer 51:46. See רַךְ No. 5 .

Pual, to be softened (a wound with ointment), Isa 1:6.

Hiphil, with לְב to break any one’s heart, Job 23:16.

Derivatives, רַךְ, רֹךְ, מֹרֶךְ.
